channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds rally against firing of federal workers, emphasizing threats to public health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of current and former federal employees rallied Wednesday outside the Department of Health and Human Services headquarters, protesting recent firings and their effect on public health, and directing much of their ire at Elon Musk and his agency's efforts to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: NATHAN ELLGREN and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: Experts say a combination of training and technology were critical to all 76 passengers and four crew members surviving the fiery Delta Air Lines plane crash in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wealthy New Jersey town pet store owner shot with crossbow after exotic bird robbery
item.description: A pet shop in New Jersey has been the subject of multiple crimes in recent weeks after its owner was shot in the face with a crossbow and an expensive bird was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cal Fire captain stabbed, killed in her home
item.description: A Cal Fire captain was fatally stabbed at her home in Ramona, California, Monday in what San Diego County Sheriff's Department officials are investigating as a suspected homicide.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE affiliate may access IRS taxpayer records: Report
item.description: An IRS employee with affiliations to the Department of Government Efficiency may be able to gain access to IRS taxpayer records.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Five cats taken to Oregon animal hospital with suspected bird flu
item.description: Three Oregon cats from the same home were taken to DoveLewis after the hospital said they were suspected of having bird flu symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Salk, NewsNation

item.title: Arizona midair collision over regional airport between Cessna, Lancair aircraft turns fatal
item.description: An aircraft collision between Cessna and a Lancair aircraft at a regional airport in Arizona on Wednesday has killed at least two people, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP: Tens of millions of dead people aren't collecting Social Security
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash: Victim's family seeks $250 million in first legal action since deadly collision
item.description: A D.C. plane crash victim's family is preparing to sue the federal government, seeking $250 million in claims after Casey Crafton, a husband and father of three, died in the collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: Southwest Airlines cutting hundreds of jobs
item.description: Southwest Airlines announced Monday that it will cut 1,750 jobs in late April in what will be the first round of layoffs in the company’s 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC voters would likely support Gov. Kathy Hochul removing Adams as mayor, AP VoteCast shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As New York Gov. Kathy Hochul weighs whether to remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, AP VoteCast shows that many New York City voters in last year's election were already on board with the move.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scammers are targeting taxpayers with this IRS text: Here's what you need to know
item.description: If you receive a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam. Criminals are impersonating the IRS to trick taxpayers into sharing personal information with them.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Colorado (AP) — Santa Rosa. Paradise. Boulder County. Lahaina. Los Angeles. All are places that have shown that American cities and their water systems weren't built to withstand wildfire, experts say. Hydrants trickled. Pumps and treatment plants lost power.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: American detained in Russia released as officials work to set up potential Trump-Putin meeting
item.description: Kalob Byers, a 28-year-old U.S. citizen detained in Russia on drug smuggling charges, was freed ahead of talks between Washington and Moscow on ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: What is a polar vortex? US gets a taste of the Arctic this week
item.description: The term “polar vortex” usually refers to the gigantic circular upper air weather pattern in the Arctic region. It is a normal pattern that is stronger in the winter and keeps some of the coldest weather bottled up near the North Pole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5
